english: not exactly 'stocking' (Sch.), but a soft, warm stuffing of the stockings; *bob-zóm* (*bob zom*) a shoe provided with such stuffing C.

pf. imp. bor (bor), 1. to throw, cast, fling, e.g. the mendicant's bowl up in the air, the sword to the ground Dzl.; zám-pai óg-tu (zam pa'i 'og tu) to precipitate a person from a bridge Dzl.; pýir (phyir) to cast out Thgy.; *ma bhor-wa ǰhé* (*ma bhor wa jhe*) C. don't throw it away! *bhor soń* (*bhor song*) I've lost it C. bor-gtór (bor gtor), bor-stór (bor stor), bor-dór (bor dor), dór-°bor-ba (dor 'bor ba) Mil. and elsewh. id.; to throw away, pour away, ču (chu) water C.; to waste, to squander Dzl. — 2. to leave, forsake, kẏim-táb (khyim thab) husband or wife Dzl.; to leave behind, mi žig bód-du (mi zhig bod du) to leave a person behind in Tibet; yáb-kyis bór-bai tse (yab kyis bor ba'i tshe) when I was left by my father, when my father died Pth.; de bór-la toń (de bor la tong) let that alone, give it up, keep away from it Mil.; *ńa lé-ka bor tań yin* (*nga las ka bor btang yin*) W. I shall now leave off working, I shall put aside my work. — 3. = °jóg-pa ('jog pa), to place, put, lay, in W. the word commonly used, in C. and B. only in certain phrases: *ǐ-ru bor* (*i ru bor*) put it here! *tán-ni ǩar bór-če* (*gdan ni dkar bor ce*) to seat a person on the carpet, to invite to a seat on the carpet; *mīi lág-tu ṭiń bór-če* (*mi'i lag tu ting bor ce*) to place a charge into somebody's hands; *nyér-pa só-ma bór-če* (*gnyer pa so ma bor ce*) to appoint a new manager; frq. with gerund: *ǩyi tág-te bór-če* (*khyi btags te bor ce*) to fasten a dog (to a chain). — 4. in particular combinations, e.g. góm-pa (gom pa).
(v. bol (bol)) cushion, bolster, mattress; snye-ból (snye bol) pillow, v. snye-ba (snye ba).

B., C., *°ból-mo* (*'bol mo*) W. 1. soft, of the ground, beds, leather, fruit etc.; soft, gentle, pliable, also as to disposition of mind; °ból-le šíg-ge sdód-pa ('bol le shig ge sdod pa) to sit still, to remain quiet, tranquil Mil. — 2. C. = mód-po (mod po).
1. v. °bo ('bo). — 2. v. °bo-ba ('bo ba). — 3. sbst. boil, bump, tumour C.
to clean, cleanse, purify Cs., °byań-kẏád ('byang khyad) custom C., W.

pf. byams (byams) Cs., to flow over, to be diffused. byam-klás-pa (byam klas pa) Lex., Cs.: unlimited, infinite; rab-°byáms (rab 'byams) Lex., Cs.: widely diffused, far spread; rab-°byáms-pa (rab 'byams pa) Cs.: a man of profound learning, a doctor of theology or philosophy; also Schr.; Köpp. II, 253.
pf. byi (byi), also pýi (phyi) and pýis (phyis), vb.n. of pýi-ba (phyi ba) to be wiped off, blotted out, effaced Cs.; to fall off, of the hair Dzl. and elsewh.

pf. byiń (bying) 1. to sink in, to sink down, to be swallowed up, šiń-rta byé-ma-la °byiń (shing rta bye ma la 'bying) Glr. the carriage sticks fast in the sand; gru čú-la (gru chu la) the ship sinks in the water Dzl. and elsewh. — 2. to grow faint, languid, remiss, rig-pa byíń-ba bsér-ba (rig pa bying ba bser ba) to lift up again one's fainting soul Mil.; byiń-rgod (bying rgod) seems to signify languor, distraction, byiń-rmúgs (bying rmugs) Mil., id., byiń-rmugs-méd-pai sgom (bying rmugs med pa'i sgom); so also byiń-tíbs (bying thibs) Lt.; sems-byiń-ba (sems bying ba) drowsiness, indolence, depression of spirits. — 3. C. *ǰhiń soń* (*jhing song*), *ǰhiń log soń* (*jhing log song*), they have dispersed, separated, are all gone home. — 4. v. ǰíń (jing), 2.

pf. byid (byid), pýid (phyid) 1. to glide, to slip Lex. = °dréd-pa ('dred pa). — 2. to disappear, to pass away, e.g. mi-tse byíd (mi tshe byid) human life passes away Lex.; in W. *tse pýid-če* (*tshe phyid ce*) vb.a., to earn a livelihood, *gár-ra čó-te* (*mgar ra bcos te*) by smith's work (C. lto zá-ba (lto za ba)).

pf. imp. pýuń (phyung), fut. (in C. also pres.) dbyuń (dbyung) Ld. *píń-če* (*phing ce*), trs. of °byuń-ba ('byung ba), to cause to come forth: 1. to take out, to remove, a pillar from its place Dzl.; *píńs(toń)* (*phings tong*) take it out (out of your pocket, out of the box etc.) Ld.; to draw out, pull out, a sword, a thorn etc., frq.; to tear out, to put out, one's eyes etc., mig dbyúń-ba dé-dag (mig dbyung ba de dag) the men whose eyes are to be put out Dzl. p. 42, 10, acc. to an emended reading; to draw forth, produce, bring to light, something that was hid Dzl. — 2. in a more gen. sense: to let proceed from, to send out, to emit, rays of light, frq.; lus-la krag (lus la khrag) to draw blood by scratching one's self Dzl.; mčí-ma (mchi ma) Glr. to shed tears; skad (skad) to make the voice to be heard, of a bird Dzl.; sdug-bsńál-gyi skad (sdug bsngal gyi skad) to utter
